title: fraxinus americana var. longifolia
intro: Fraxinus americana var. longifolia, also known as the American green ash, is a deciduous tree that is native to eastern North America. It is a variant of the American ash tree, characterized by its long leaves. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus americana var. longifolia include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are derived from its bark and roots. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and to provide relief from pain and inflammation, ultimately promoting overall health and well-being.
